title: GLib-ERROR ... gthread-posix.c: line 1339 (g_system_thread_new): error 'Operation not permitted' during 'pthread_create'
body:
**Description**

The daily Cirrus-cron runs on the `release-1.22` and `release-1.23` are consistently failing the F33 `copy-preserving-extended-attributes` test beginning on November 16th.  No runs prior to this date produced this error.  The tests pass under F34.  The issue does not appear to affect tests run on the `main` branch.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. In a PR or directly on one of the affected branches
2. Run ***Integration fedora-33 w/ vfs*** -and/or- ***Integration fedora-33 w/ overly***

**Describe the results you received:**

([source task log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5995611200684032?logs=integration_test#L1114))

```
[+1118s] not ok 335 copy-preserving-extended-attributes
...cut...
[+1118s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah from --quiet --signature-policy /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./policy.json registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal
[+1118s] # fedora-minimal-working-container
[+1118s] # $ /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./../bin/buildah run fedora-minimal-working-container microdnf -y install /usr/bin/getfattr /usr/bin/setfattr /usr/sbin/setcap
[+1118s] # 
[+1118s] # (microdnf:1): GLib-ERROR **: 17:08:59.317: file ../glib/gthread-posix.c: line 1339 (g_system_thread_new): error 'Operation not permitted' during 'pthread_create'
[+1118s] # container exited on trace/breakpoint trap
[+1118s] # error while running runtime: exit status 1
...cut...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Prior to Nov. 16th this test was passing on these branches.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

Built from source

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.22.4-dev

```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

comments.body: Please update the version of containers-common in the VM to get an updated version of the default seccomp filter configuration (`/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`).  The current version for Fedora 33 is `containers-common-1-20.fc33`.
Can we just drop Fedora 33 testing?

Reminder: This is only an issue (some how suddenly) on the two release branches, it doesn't affect `main`.

> Please update the version of containers-common in the VM 

I really hate to do that on release branches, those environments are suppose to be long-term stable in order to support any future backport work.

> Can we just drop Fedora 33 testing?

Maybe?  Again these are release branches, so it probably depends on their age...which seems is "not that old".  Though it's really up to you.  Is the risk of a disabled-test missing something on a backport worth the effort to maintain the daily runs?

In any case...this all begs the question: How the heck did these tests start spewing this error suddenly, on (supposedly) unchanging release branch CI environments?  That's what's got my eyebrows in a bunch (and one ear raised).
F33 is dead, drop it from the tests.

... as of 30 November.  This is a general problem of images using syscalls that we didn't know we'd need to allow in the default seccomp profile when the profile was last revised for an earlier release.
okay, those are the magic words :smile:

> This is a general problem of images using syscalls that we didn't know we'd need

BTW, @nalind testing really should only depend on static/fixed/unchanging inputs, including container images.  For example, tests should avoid using `latest` images, use a version tag instead unless the image is known to be kept static (like the ones we maintain copies of in quay).  That's how this problem can be avoided (in the future).

There's an exception case I'm aware of with `quay.io/libpod/fedora-minimal:latest` that needs taking care of.  I'll open a separate PR for that.
